content.full_text:
September 21, 1990

John Wallace, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter seeks written confirmation of the oral advice you gave me during the week of September 4, 1990, regarding the application of the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act to financial transactions involving sub-sub contractors.

Specifically, you had informed me that the FPPC does not normally consider a real estate developer to be a "source of income" to a self-employed architect when the architect is hired, reviewed and paid by a sub-contractor, unless other facts connect the developer to the architect. Only the sub-contractor who actually employed the architect would normally be considered a source of income for purposes of Government Code section 87103.

As examples of the type of facts that would render the developer a source of the architect's income, you listed substantive review of the architect's work, the power to hire or fire the architect, paychecks signed by the developer, etc. None of these facts, or any others that would tend to show that the architect was under the developer's direction or control, exist in the present situation.

As I mentioned to you on the telephone, the architect in our situation is even farther removed from the developer because the sub-contractor who hired the architect is in fact under the direction and control of another sub-contractor.

If this letter does not accurately portray the factual situation as you understood it, or the advice that you gave, please call me for clarification.

John Wallace, Esq.
September 21, 1990
Page 2

Thank you very much.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr
5259.01
